ops.title: capt-hook: exempt PEP 723 script-metadata fences from the verbose-comment hook
ops.description: The general-pack comments hook (captain_hook/packs/general/comments.py) counts PEP 723 inline-metadata blocks as plain comment runs (Python never classifies comment runs as docs), so any new/touched script whose `# /// script` … `# ///` fence exceeds 3 lines — i.e. any script with `dependencies`, and especially one with `[tool.uv.sources]` — is blocked as "Verbose comment". Fix in captain-hook: exempt comment blocks that are a well-formed PEP 723 fence (first row `# /// script` (or `# /// <type>`), last row `# ///`, every row `#`-prefixed) from the budget — e.g. filter them in comments.py `touched()` — with regression tests: a 6-line `# /// script` fence with uv.sources → Allow; an equal-length non-fence run → still Block. Then release (push tag; github-release job cuts it) so the fleet's dispatcher picks it up. Follow-up: switch cc-skills tools/guides-deploy-keys.py from its sys.path.insert bridge back to the intended `dependencies = ["fleetlib"]` + `[tool.uv.sources] { path = "fleetlib", editable = true }` header once live.

ops.text: capt-hook test passes with a regression fixture: 6-line PEP 723 fence Write → Allow, same-size plain comment run → Block
captain-hook release cut and live dispatch (uv run capt-hook run PreToolUse from checkout) allows the fence
tools/guides-deploy-keys.py in cc-skills migrated from sys.path.insert to the [tool.uv.sources] fleetlib path dep and still runs (--list)
